title: What are the options for monitoring my

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: Thanks, Chris. Right now I've got a three different brands of repeaters and am looking at a custom build, too.
messages.content_html: Thanks, Chris. Right now I've got a three different brands of repeaters and am looking at a custom build, too.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: it's not so much "brand" as "chip family". ESP32 vs nRF.
messages.content_html: it's not so much "brand" as "chip family". ESP32 vs nRF.

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: I also understand that Let's Mesh can be connected to a MeshCore-HA setup. It wasn't clear to me how that would work because I thought Let's Mesh wanted to be connected directly to a repeater and listen to all traffic through it.
messages.content_html: I also understand that Let's Mesh can be connected to a MeshCore-HA setup. It wasn't clear to me how that would work because I thought Let's Mesh wanted to be connected directly to a repeater and listen to all traffic through it.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: but if you have a fleet of repeaters, LetsMesh probably isn't the way to go. You might run it on one of them that is at your "base" location. But I presume the others are remote to extend coverage, and thus would be difficult to get MQTT back from. So in that case, the Home Assistent integration is probably your main option. Just make sure to set it to only query once every 15 minutes or so so you don't flood the mesh with your query traffic.
messages.content_html: but if you have a fleet of repeaters, LetsMesh probably isn't the way to go. You might run it on one of them that is at your "base" location. But I presume the others are remote to extend coverage, and thus would be difficult to get MQTT back from. So in that case, the Home Assistent integration is probably your main option. Just make sure to set it to only query once every 15 minutes or so so you don't flood the mesh with your query traffic.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: They are all nRF. SeeedStudio SenseCap Solar Node P1 - XIAO nRF52840 Plus RAK WisMesh Repeater Mini - Nordic nRF52840 ThinkNode M6 - nRF52840 Custom one was going to use XIAO nRF52840 & Wio-SX1262 Kit
messages.content_html: They are all nRF. SeeedStudio SenseCap Solar Node P1 - XIAO nRF52840 Plus RAK WisMesh Repeater Mini - Nordic nRF52840 ThinkNode M6 - nRF52840 Custom one was going to use XIAO nRF52840 & Wio-SX1262 Kit

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: LetsMesh doesn't need to be on a repeater directly (although there are some benefits to that, because the repeater provides SNR/RSSI for the received packets). For example, I have an nRF-based repeater that is my real repeater. I then run a Heltec V4 (which is ESP32-based) as a dedicated observer (so repeats are turned off on it). It basically hears all the re-broadcasts from my real repeater to feed into LetsMesh.
messages.content_html: LetsMesh doesn't need to be on a repeater directly (although there are some benefits to that, because the repeater provides SNR/RSSI for the received packets). For example, I have an nRF-based repeater that is my real repeater. I then run a Heltec V4 (which is ESP32-based) as a dedicated observer (so repeats are turned off on it). It basically hears all the re-broadcasts from my real repeater to feed into LetsMesh.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Clinton
messages.content_html: Thanks, @Christopher A. Wichura.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: In our region, there are a couple people running Home Assistant to a USB companion device. In addition to HA querying their repeaters that they've spread around, it is also configured to feed into LetsMesh.
messages.content_html: In our region, there are a couple people running Home Assistant to a USB companion device. In addition to HA querying their repeaters that they've spread around, it is also configured to feed into LetsMesh.
messages.reactions_html: 1
